title: Secure system command construction
description: Constructing system command arguments from user input without proper validation creates command injection vulnerabilities. Attackers can manipulate command arguments to execute arbitrary commands or access unauthorized system resources.
recommendation: Validate and sanitize all user inputs used in system commands, or use parameterized command execution.

noncompliant.description: Using unsanitized user input in system commands allows attackers to inject malicious commands and execute arbitrary code.

compliant.description: Validating, sanitizing, and using allowlists for command parameters prevents command injection attacks while maintaining functionality.

checks.message: Potential command injection vulnerability - avoid constructing system commands with user input
